Details: One study cited reduction of 4-nonylphenol by using biosorption and integrated sonochemical Fenton reaction followed by biosorption treatment. Sonochemical Fenton degradation experiments were conducted at 300 kHz in homemade reactor. Biosorption treatment experiments were conducted using P. lilacinus fungal strains. Both the methods were effective in removing 4-nonylphenol, with biosorption removing 59 to 61 percent in seven days and integrated sonochemical Fenton reaction followed by biosorption achieved up to 88 percent removal.  Sonochemical Fenton reaction removed 66 percent of 4-nonylphenol in the first hour. No hazardous byproducts were detected in the experiments.
